rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CHOY VS. CAROLE ROBERTSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 7, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANTS CAROLE ROBERTSON and EUGENE ROBERTSON's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. Opposing party failed to provide courtesy copies as required by Local Rule 2.6B, and as was required by the Court's previous order of December 17, 2013 when this matter was continued to January 7, 2013 to give opposing party an opportunity to provide courtesy copies of the opposing papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA LOPEZ RIVERA VS. THE FINEST JEWELRY.COM INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 7, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARIA RIVERA's Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs. The motion for fees and costs is granted. Plaintiff is awarded $57,600 as reasonable attorney's fees and costs of $3,195.45. The Court did not apportion the fees for the fraud and CLRA work because the claims raise common issues that are not severable. The Court denies the request for a multiplier. The lodestar was $450.00/hr. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LAW OFFICE OF IAN P. ZIMMERMAN APC VS. ASHWIN LADVA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arba And For Appointment Of Arbitrator
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 7, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF LAW OFFICE OF IAN P. ZIMMERMAN APC's Petition To Compel Arbitration And For Appointment Of Arbitrator. The motion is denied. Plaintiff fails to prove the existence of an agreement to arbitrate between the parties. Plaintiff also fails to cite any authority that allows this Court to order non-party Mirth Estrada to arbitrate her fee dispute with plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JAMES POWELL VS. FOP DEVELOPMENT GROUP LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 7, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs FOP DEVELOPMENT GROUP LLC, GREEN POWER GROUP LLC, PATRICK MCKAY, KARRIE BERCIK, GARY TAORMINA, WAYNE SCHAFNIT's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Action. Denied, without prejudice. The copies of the Second Amended FOP Development Group, LLC Operating Agreement and Third Amended FOP Development Group, LLC Operating Agreements are unsigned. (Bercik Decl., Exhibits B, C.) Further, the FOP Development Group, LLC Purchase and Subscription Agreement, which incorporates the Operating Agreement as Schedule 2, is not attached to the Agreement. (Id. at Exhibit A.) Defendants' introduction of evidence in reply is improper and is not considered by the court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
